sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: Διόμηδες , ἢ σὺ κτεῖνε Θρῄκιον λεών ,
sections.translation: Diomedes , either you slay the Thracian folk , or leave that to me , while your care must be the horses .

sections.label: Section 624
sections.original: ἐγὼ φονεύσω , πωλοδαμνήσεις δὲ σύ ·
sections.translation: I will do the killing , and you master the horses .

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: τρίβων γὰρ εἶ τὰ κομψὰ καὶ νοεῖν σοφός .
sections.translation: For you are well versed in clever tricks , and have a ready wit . And it is right to station a man where he may best serve .

sections.label: Section 627
sections.original: καὶ μὴν καθʼ ἡμᾶς τόνδʼ Ἀλέξανδρον βλέπω
sections.translation: Look ! there I see Paris coming towards us ; perhaps he has heard from the guard a vague rumor that foes are near .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: πότερα σὺν ἄλλοις ἢ μόνος πορεύεται ;
sections.translation: Are others with him or does he come alone ?

sections.label: Section 631
sections.original: μόνος · πρὸς εὐνὰς δʼ , ὡς ἔοικεν , Ἕκτορος
sections.translation: Alone ; to Hector ’ s couch he seems to wend his way , to announce to him that spies are in the camp .

sections.label: Section 633
sections.original: οὐκ οὖν ὑπάρχειν τόνδε κατθανόντα χρή ;
sections.translation: Ought he not head the list of slain ?

sections.label: Section 634
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν δύναιο τοῦ πεπρωμένου πλέον .
sections.translation: You can not overreach destiny .

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: τοῦτον δὲ πρὸς σῆς χειρὸς οὐ θέμις θανεῖν .
sections.translation: It is not decreed that he should fall by your hand . But hasten on your mission of fore-ordained slaughter , while I , feigning to be Cypris , his ally , and to aid him in his efforts , will answer the foe with unsound words .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτʼ ἐγὼ μὲν εἶπον · ὃν δὲ χρὴ παθεῖν ,
sections.translation: I tell you this ; but the fated victim does not know , nor has he heard , for all he is so near . Exeunt Odysseus and Diomedes .

sections.label: Section 642
sections.original: σὲ τὸν στρατηγὸν καὶ κασίγνητον λέγω ,
sections.translation: To you I call , general and brother , Hector , are you asleep ? Should you not awake ? Some enemy draws near our army ,

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: ἢ κλῶπες ἄνδρες ἢ κατάσκοποί τινες .
sections.translation: or thieves perhaps , or spies .

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: θάρσει · φυλάσσει σʼ ἥδε πρευμενὴς Κύπρις .
sections.translation: Courage ! See , Cypris watches over you in gracious mood . Your warfare is my concern , for I do not forget the honor you once did me , and I thank you for your good service . And now , when the army of Troy is triumphant ,

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: ἥκω πορεύουσʼ ἄνδρα σοι μέγαν φίλον ,
sections.translation: I have come bringing to you a powerful friend , the Thracian child of the Muse , the heavenly singer ; his father ’ s name is Strymon .

sections.label: Section 653
sections.original: αἰεί ποτʼ εὖ φρονοῦσα τυγχάνεις πόλει
sections.translation: Always to this city and to me you are a kind friend , and I am sure that decision I then made

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: κρίνας σέ φημι τῇδε προσθέσθαι πόλει .
sections.translation: conferred you upon this city , the highest treasure life affords . I came when I heard a vague report — for a rumor prevailed amlng the guards — that Achaean spies are here . One man , that did not see them , says so , while another , that saw them come , cannot describe them ;

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: ὧν οὕνεκʼ εὐνὰς ἤλυθον πρὸς Ἕκτορος .
sections.translation: and so I am on my way to Hector ’ s tent .

sections.label: Section 661
sections.original: μηδὲν φοβηθῇς · οὐδὲν ἐν στρατῷ νέον ·
sections.translation: Fear nothing ; all is quiet in the army , and Hector has gone to assign a sleeping-place to the Thracian army .

sections.label: Section 663
sections.original: σύ τοί με πείθεις , σοῖς δὲ πιστεύων λόγοις
sections.translation: You persuade me , and I believe your words , and will go to guard my post , free of fear .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: χώρει · μέλειν γὰρ πάντʼ ἐμοὶ δόκει τὰ σά ,
sections.translation: Go , for it is my pleasure ever to watch your interests , that so I may see my allies prosperous . Yes , and you too shall recognize my zeal . Exit Paris .

sections.label: Section 668
sections.original: ὑμᾶς δʼ ἀυτῶ τοὺς ἄγαν ἐρρωμένους ,
sections.translation: In a loud voice , to Odysseus and Diomedes . Son of Laertes , I bid you sheath your whetted swords , you warriors all too keen .

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: κεῖται γὰρ ἡμῖν Θρῄκιος στρατηλάτης ,
sections.translation: For the Thracian chief lies dead and his horses are captured , but the enemy know it , and are coming against you ; fly with all speed to the ships ’ station . Why delay saving your lives , when the enemy ’ s storm is just bursting on you ?

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: — ἔα ἔα ·
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! At them , at them ! Strike them , strike them !

sections.label: Section 678
sections.original: — τίς ἁνήρ ; λεύσσετε · τοῦτον αὐδῶ .
sections.translation: Who goes there ?

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: — δεῦρο δεῦρο πᾶς .
sections.translation: Come here , here , everyone ! I have them , I have clutched them fast .

sections.label: Section 682
sections.original: — τίς ὁ λόχος ; πόθεν ἔβας ; ποδαπὸς εἶ ;
sections.translation: What is your company ? Where did you come from ? Who are you ?

sections.label: Section 683
sections.original: οὔ σε χρὴ εἰδέναι · θανῇ γὰρ σήμερον δράσας κακῶς .
sections.translation: It is not for you to know ; for you will die today for your villainy .

sections.label: Section 684
sections.original: οὐκ ἐρεῖς ξύνθημα , λόγχην πρὶν διὰ στέρνων μολεῖν ;
sections.translation: Will you not declare the password , before my sword finds its way to your heart ?

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: ἵστω . θάρσει .
sections.translation: Halt ! Be of good heart .

sections.label: Section 685b
sections.original: πέλας ἴθι . παῖε πᾶς .
sections.translation: Come near . Strike , everyone !

sections.label: Section 686
sections.original: ἦ σὺ δὴ Ῥῆσον κατέκτας ;
sections.translation: What ! have you slain Rhesus ?

sections.label: Section 686b
sections.original: ἀλλὰ τὸν κτενοῦντα σὲ ⋯
sections.translation: No , but you , who came to slay us —

sections.label: Section 687
sections.original: ἴσχε πᾶς τις .
sections.translation: Stay , every man of you !

sections.label: Section 687b
sections.original: οὐ μὲν οὖν .
sections.translation: No , no , lay on !

sections.label: Section 687c
sections.original: ἆ · φίλιον ἄνδρα μὴ θένῃς .
sections.translation: Ah ! do not slay a friend !

sections.label: Section 688
sections.original: καὶ τί δὴ τὸ σῆμα ;
sections.translation: What is the password , then ?

sections.label: Section 688b
sections.original: Φοῖβος .
sections.translation: Phoebus .

sections.label: Section 688c
sections.original: ἔμαθον · ἴσχε πᾶς δόρυ .
sections.translation: Right ! every man hold back his spear !

sections.label: Section 689
sections.original: οἶσθʼ ὅποι βεβᾶσιν ἅνδρες ;
sections.translation: Do you know where the men have gone ?

sections.label: Section 689b
sections.original: τῇδέ πῃ κατείδομεν .
sections.translation: Somewhere here I caught a sight of them .

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: ἕρπω πᾶς κατʼ ἴχνος αὐτῶν . ἢ βοὴν ἐγερτέον ;
sections.translation: Close on their track each man of you ! Or should we shout for aid ?

sections.label: Section 691
sections.original: ἀλλὰ συμμάχους ταράσσειν δεινὸν ἐκ νυκτῶν φόβῳ .
sections.translation: [*] No , it would be strange conduct to disturb our friends with wild alarms by night . Exeunt Odysseus and Diomedes .

sections.label: Section 1.692
sections.original: — τίς ἀνδρῶν ὁ βάς ;
sections.translation: Who was that man who slipped away ? Who was he that will loudly boast his daring in escaping me ?

sections.label: Section 1.695
sections.original: πόθεν νιν κυρήσω ;
sections.translation: How shall I catch him now ? To whom shall I liken him , the man who came by night with fearless step passing through our ranks and the guard we set ? Is he a Thessalian or

sections.label: Section 1.700
sections.original: παραλίαν Λοκρῶν νεμόμενος πόλιν ;
sections.translation: a dweller in some seacoast town of Locris ? Or does he make his living among the islands scattered in the sea ? Who was he ? Where from ? What is his fatherland ? What god does he avow as lord of all the rest ?

sections.label: Section 1.704
sections.original: — ἆρʼ ἔστʼ Ὀδυσσέως τοὔργον ἢ τίνος τόδε ;
sections.translation: Whose work is this ? is it the deed of Odysseus ?

sections.label: Section 1.705
sections.original: εἰ τοῖς πάροιθε χρὴ τεκμαίρεσθαι · τί μήν ;
sections.translation: If one may conjecture from his former acts , of course it is .

sections.label: Section 1.706
sections.original: — δοκεῖς γάρ ; — τί μὴν οὔ ;
sections.translation: Do you think so really ?

sections.label: Section 1.707
sections.original: — θρασὺς γοῦν ἐς ἡμᾶς .
sections.translation: He is a bold foe for us .

sections.label: Section 1.708
sections.original: — τίνʼ ἀλκήν ; τίνʼ αἰνεῖς ; — Ὀδυσσῆ .
sections.translation: What strength ? Whom are you praising ?

sections.label: Section 1.709
sections.original: — μὴ κλωπὸς αἴνει φωτὸς αἱμύλον δόρυ .
sections.translation: Do not praise the crafty weapons that a robber uses .

sections.label: Section 1.710
sections.original: ἔβα καὶ πάρος
sections.translation: Once before he came into this city , with swimming bleary eyes , clad in rags and tatters , his sword hidden in his cloak .

sections.label: Section 1.715
sections.original: βίον δʼ ἐπαιτῶν εἷρπʼ ἀγύρτης τις λάτρις ,
sections.translation: And like some vagrant menial he slunk about begging his living , his head rough and dirty ; and he spoke bitterly of the royal house of the Atreidae — as though he were really opposed to those chiefs !

sections.label: Section 1.720
sections.original: ὄλοιτʼ ὄλοιτο πανδίκως ,
sections.translation: Would , oh ! would he had perished , as was his due , before he set foot on Phrygia ’ s soil !

sections.label: Section 1.722
sections.original: — εἴτʼ οὖν Ὀδυσσέως εἴτε μή , φόβος μʼ ἔχει ·
sections.translation: Whether it was really Odysseus or not , I am afraid ;

sections.label: Section 1.723
sections.original: Ἕκτωρ γὰρ ἡμῖν τοῖς φύλαξι μέμψεται .
sections.translation: for Hector will blame us sentinels .

sections.label: Section 1.724
sections.original: — τί λάσκων ; — δυσοίζων .
sections.translation: What can he allege ?

sections.label: Section 1.725
sections.original: — τί δρᾶσαι ; τί ταρβεῖς ;
sections.translation: What have we done ? Why are you afraid ?

sections.label: Section 1.726
sections.original: — καθʼ ἡμᾶς περᾶσαι ⋯ — τίνʼ ἀνδρῶν ;
sections.translation: They got past us —

sections.label: Section 1.727
sections.original: — οἳ τῆσδε νυκτὸς ἦλθον ἐς Φρυγῶν στρατόν .
sections.translation: The ones who came this night to the Phrygian army .
